name: DOGE Session Drive Continuation v1
thesis: DOGE 4h is the live primary lane for testing continuation after a structurally dominant drive, interpreted here as a broad impulse-and-hold sequence rather than literal intraday session timing. The edge should come from follow-through after an accepted directional burst, not from single-bar breakout chasing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if drive-and-hold setups remain too sparse, if persistence and hold filters conflict, or if continuation fails to clear costs in blind windows.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional burst establishes local dominance and the next bar(s) hold enough structure for continuation instead of immediate mean reversion.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in mostly choppy DOGE 4h conditions, occasional impulse bursts can persist briefly when the initial retrace fails to reclaim prior balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the current primary lane and still plausible for a broadened drive-continuation thesis because DOGE often overreacts enough to create visible hold/fail behavior on 4h bars.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If DOGE remains purely mean reverting without brief persistence bursts, hold entries will degrade into chop.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries occur but persistence is too short, shorten the holding horizon and de-risk faster before tightening the trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; momentum improvement and structural hold are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad drive-hold definitions rather than exact opening-range rules
density_sanity_check.asset_timeframe_mismatch: acceptable with a broadened drive interpretation

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
